concept: Stable / Not Likely to Change / Firmly Established
description: Not likely to give way, become unbalanced, or change; firmly established. 'Mureo' names the quality of secure foundation—structures that hold, systems that persist, conditions that can be relied upon.
sound_symbolism: The word grounds and holds. 'Mu' begins with grounded earth, foundation set. Then 're' remains firm, structure maintained. Finally 'o' opens with solidity, stability complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'mureo muila' (stable earth). Related to 'kolu' (steady) and 'luwi' (flexible)—stability, steadiness, and flexibility together create resilient systems.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Stable communities can plan for futures—social stability enables long-term thinking and sustainable choices.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Stable structures lasted generations—traditional building created stability through understanding materials and forces.
